meta.window: Last 12 hours (6 AM to 6 PM PDT)

posts.title: FB1750 - Rear
posts.text: Hey guys, what rear wing are you riding your FB1750 with? I am mainly looking into Long Distance Pumping either from dock or via flatwater paddle-up as the main disciplines! Advance+ Psychoshort Fatty Fuse and 80cm Fatty Mast. Rider: 72kg

comments.author: Yannick Favieres
comments.text: For me, the same setup. I'm currently 98 kg, and I'm using the 358/25.

comments.author: Michael Kihn
comments.text: @Yannick Favieres My Skinnies are all part of other setups and I got the Surf Skinny 300/45 and 280/43 lying around. Wondering if they could be a good fit? Anybody tried out that combo?

comments.author: William Vosburgh
comments.text: 358/25 on Advance+ Psychoshort Fatty Fuse and 80cm Fatty Mast. 68 kg. First and only Axis setup. Far more pitch sensitive than anything else I've ridden.

comments.author: Tom La Majorette
comments.text: I have 1500, I'm riding that in 40cm2. I guess with 1750, it's will be great too

comments.author: Deon Aumaier
comments.text: Great Post! I have the very similar kit. FB 1750, 80cm FATTY Mast. Except I went w/FATTY Ultrashort fuse. My stab, skinny 358/30. I'm curious about the super short fuse. Easier to turn? The 1750 is already so pitch sensitive even with ultrashort?
comments.notable: FB1750 pitch sensitivity issue even with ultrashort fuse

comments.author: Peter Michl
comments.text: 30 for dockstarts. 40 or 45 for flatwater paddle ups. No shims. 90kg dry.

key_insights: FB1750 + Advance+ Psychoshort Fuse + 80cm Fatty Mast is a common pump setup
Multiple riders report FB1750 is very pitch sensitive
Stab size split: 358/25 popular for heavier riders (68-98kg), 30cm for dock starts, 40-45 for flatwater
Surf Skinny 300/45 and 280/43 mentioned as potential alternatives

active_posts_recent_comments.title: Comparison of Axis foils - Which one is which? (Tempo 1090 vs Fireball 1070)
active_posts_recent_comments.recent_comment: А какой ты использовал говорили что на 1350 катал (Translation: What did you use, they said you rode the 1350)
active_posts_recent_comments.key_info: Community quiz post - Fireball 1070 (left) vs Tempo 1090 (right). Same wingspan, Tempo less draggy. Community answer: Fireball 1070 left, Tempo 1090 right.

active_posts_recent_comments.title: Mast Size Question - 820mm vs 750mm

notable_new_comments.author: Jimmy Jam Foils
notable_new_comments.text: 82cm is good for behind the boat. Sweet spot range is 75~82cm. I teach with 75cm but once you know how to foil 80~82 best for boat wake. Longer allows harder carving. For beach starts shorter is good. I have 75cm, 82cm, 105cm in 19mm aluminum and 72cm, 80cm, 90cm UHM carbon available to demo.
notable_new_comments.notable: Dealer/instructor has extensive mast inventory to demo

notable_new_comments.author: Mark Shinn
notable_new_comments.text: Not sure that the 75 is a great option for winging. I think 82 is the go to with zero hesitation.
notable_new_comments.notable: AXIS team/partner Mark Shinn endorses 820mm for wing

active_posts_recent_comments.key_consensus: 820mm mast overwhelmingly preferred over 750mm by community and experts

active_posts_recent_comments.title: Surge vs Spitfire for waves, Gold Coast - 85kg

notable_new_comments.author: Leonardo Loppi
notable_new_comments.text: Sean Richardson do you think Surge (830 or 890) is a good foil in messy washing wavy water? Or Spit still better?

active_posts_recent_comments.title: Wingfoiling on Swan River, WA Australia
active_posts_recent_comments.text: Making the most of the wind season here in WA. Now looking for some storm winds in the next few months! Loving wing foiling in the sawn river! Via Instagram: Catching some air on the Swan River with my trusty Axis and Duotone gear! #Wingfoiling #swanriver #australia #axis #duotone

active_posts_recent_comments.title: SURF FX video - What's new at Axis with Kai Thompson
active_posts_recent_comments.text: They latest race, surf and dock Axis gear with international team rider Kai Thompson. YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jOgkjAQkHpo

active_posts_recent_comments.title: AXIS Setup Survey - Results from 150 riders
active_posts_recent_comments.text: We surveyed nearly 150 AXIS riders across 15+ countries to find out what they're riding, how they set it up, and what they'd change. The results are in, and some of them surprised us. Want your setup counted? Takes 3 minutes.
active_posts_recent_comments.notable: Cross-posted from AXIS Foils official page

posts_last_24h.title: AXIS Setup Survey - What's Your Favorite AXIS Setup?
posts_last_24h.text: We surveyed nearly 150 AXIS riders across 15+ countries to find out what they're riding, how they set it up, and what they'd change.

unanswered_questions.author: Katrin Kolo
unanswered_questions.text: Thanks for this survey. I answered already and would be interested what percentage of answers is female riders (in each discipline if possible and especially dockstarting). Thanks!

notable_observations: FB1750 pitch sensitivity is a recurring theme - multiple riders mention it
820mm mast strong consensus winner over 750mm with both community and AXIS partner Mark Shinn
Surge vs Spitfire debate active, particularly for messy wave conditions
Female rider representation question raised by Top Fan Katrin Kolo - unanswered by AXIS
SURF FX active dealer relationship evident - sharing Kai Thompson content
